import pytest
from testcontainers.postgres import PostgresContainer
from testcontainers.core.waiting_utils import wait_for_logs
import psycopg

EXPECTED = 7

def count_rows(conn, table):
    with conn.cursor() as cur:
        cur.execute(f"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {table}")
        return cur.fetchone()[0]

@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def postgres_fixture():
    pg = PostgresContainer("postgres:15")
    pg.start()
    # BUG: wait_for_logs sees the FIRST occurrence of "ready to accept connections"
    # which actually happens during init before the superuser is set up.
    # Tests race ahead and connect before the real bootstrap is done.
    wait_for_logs(pg, "database system is ready to accept connections", timeout=30)
    conn = psycopg.connect(pg.get_connection_url())
    conn.autocommit = True
    with conn.cursor() as cur:
        for name in ["a","b","c","d","e","f","g"]:
            cur.execute("INSERT INTO users(name) VALUES (%s) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING", (name,))
    yield conn
    conn.close(); pg.stop()

def test_user_count_after_seed(postgres_fixture):
    conn = postgres_fixture
    n = count_rows(conn, "users")
    assert n == EXPECTED, f"expected {EXPECTED} rows after seed, got {n}"
expected_fix_hint: Wait for the SECOND "ready to accept connections" log line, or poll SELECT 1 until it succeeds; tests should not run before pg_init completes.
